game_info.title: Bubble-Cash-With-Different-Physical-Properties
game_info.concept:
{
  "game_name": "Bubble Cash",
  "genre": "Casino",
  "core_mechanics": "The game involves a grid of colored bubbles at the top of the screen and a player-controlled shooter at the bottom. The player aims a projectile bubble, matching it with three or more adjacent bubbles of the same color. 

**Player Actions:**
-   **Aiming:** The player aims the shooter by moving the mouse left and right or using keyboard arrow keys. A laser guide line indicates the trajectory of the projectile bubble, reflecting off walls. The guide updates dynamically based on the current aiming angle.
-   **Firing:** The player releases the mouse button (or presses Spacebar) to launch the current projectile bubble towards the aimed direction.

**Rules and Systems:**
-   **Bubble Grid:** Bubbles are arranged in a hexagonal grid pattern at the top of the screen. Each level starts with a pre-arranged, static layout of bubbles.
-   **Projectile Bubbles:** The shooter holds the 'current' bubble to fire and displays the 'next' bubble in line. Bubbles are drawn from a limited, level-specific pool of colors present on the board.
-   **Collision Detection (Matter.js):** When a projectile bubble is fired, it moves in a straight line until it collides with a wall or another bubble. Matter.js will handle rigid body physics for collisions and bouncing.
-   **Attachment Logic:** Upon collision with an existing bubble, the projectile bubble snaps into the nearest available grid position, becoming part of the static bubble grid. Its color is then checked against its neighbors.
-   **Matching and Popping:** If the newly attached bubble creates a cluster of three or more contiguous bubbles of the same color, all bubbles in that cluster (and any other clusters of 3+ of the same color attached to it) pop and are removed from the board. 
-   **Detachment:** If popping bubbles causes any remaining bubbles to become unattached from the top of the screen or any other bubbles that *are* attached to the top, those detached bubbles will fall due to gravity (Matter.js) and be removed from the board, awarding bonus points.
-   **Time Limit:** Each level has a specific time limit. A visible timer counts down during gameplay.

**State Tracking:**
-   `currentLevel`: Integer indicating the active level.
-   `score`: Integer representing the player's current score.
-   `timerRemaining`: Float indicating seconds left in the current level.
-   `bubbleGrid`: A 2D array or similar data structure storing bubble objects (color, position, radius, Matter.js body reference) for the static board.
-   `projectileBubble`: Object representing the bubble currently loaded in the shooter (color, Matter.js body reference).
-   `nextBubble`: Object representing the bubble to be loaded after the `projectileBubble` is fired.
-   `shooterAngle`: Float representing the current aiming angle of the shooter.
-   `gameState`: Enum (`MENU`, `PLAYING`, `LEVEL_TRANSITION`, `WIN`, `LOSE`, `GAME_OVER`).
-   `highScores`: Array of objects for local high scores (name, score).",
  "visual_elements": "**Game Objects:**
-   **Bubbles:** Rendered as `p5.js` `ellipse()` shapes. Each bubble has a distinct fill color (e.g., Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, Purple, Orange). All bubbles will have a consistent diameter (e.g., 40px). When popping, they will briefly scale down and fade out (`p5.js` `tint()` or `alpha` modification).
-   **Shooter:** A simple `p5.js` `triangle()` or `rect()` at the bottom center of the screen, indicating the launch point. Its base remains fixed, while its tip rotates with the aiming angle.
-   **Aiming Laser Guide:** A `p5.js` `line()` drawn from the shooter's tip, reflecting off the left and right walls (and potentially other bubbles for more advanced aiming) to show the projectile's path. It will be a thin, semi-transparent line (e.g., `stroke(255, 255, 255, 100)`).
-   **Walls:** Invisible boundaries for Matter.js to handle collisions, but visually represented by the screen edges.
-   **Background:** A simple gradient or solid color `p5.js` background.

**UI Elements:**
-   **Score Display:** Text rendered in the top-left corner. Font: `Arial` or `monospace`, size: `24px`, color: `white`. Format: `SCORE: XXXXX`.
-   **Timer Display:** Text rendered in the top-center. Font: `Arial` or `monospace`, size: `24px`, color: `white`. Format: `TIME: MM:SS`.
-   **Level Indicator:** Text rendered in the top-right corner. Font: `Arial` or `monospace`, size: `24px`, color: `white`. Format: `LEVEL: X`.
-   **Next Bubble Preview:** A smaller `ellipse()` shape displayed next to the shooter, showing the color of the bubble that will be fired next.
-   **Game State Messages:** Large, centered text for 'LEVEL COMPLETE!', 'GAME OVER', 'YOU WIN!', 'YOU LOSE!'. Font: `Arial Black`, size: `48px`, color: `yellow` or `red`.
-   **Main Menu Buttons/Text:** Clear `p5.js` `rect()` or `text()` elements for 'START GAME', 'INSTRUCTIONS', etc.",
  "control_scheme": "The game is designed with a primary mouse-based control scheme, but is mapped to keyboard controls for HTML5 desktop play as requested:

-   **Mouse Movement (X-axis):** Controls the aiming angle of the shooter. Moving the mouse left or right rotates the laser guide.
-   **Mouse Click (Release):** Fires the current projectile bubble in the direction of the laser guide upon release of the left mouse button.

**Keyboard Controls:**
-   **ArrowLeft:** Rotates the shooter and aiming guide counter-clockwise (to the left).
-   **ArrowRight:** Rotates the shooter and aiming guide clockwise (to the right).
-   **Space:** Fires the current projectile bubble.
-   **Z:** (Optional) Swaps the current projectile bubble with the 'next bubble' in the queue. This can be used once per bubble fired.",
  "game_states": "The game progresses through several distinct states:

1.  **MAIN_MENU:**
    -   **Description:** Displays the game title, 'Start Game' button, and 'Instructions' (optional).
    -   **Transition Trigger:** Player clicks 'Start Game' button.
    -   **Transition To:** `PLAYING` (starts Level 1).

2.  **PLAYING:**
    -   **Description:** Active gameplay state. Bubbles are on screen, timer is counting down, player can aim and fire. Score, Timer, and Level are displayed.
    -   **Transition Triggers:**
        -   All bubbles on the board are cleared.
        -   Timer reaches 0 and bubbles remain on the board.
        -   Bubbles cross the 'lose line' at the bottom of the screen.
    -   **Transition To:**
        -   `LEVEL_TRANSITION` (if all bubbles cleared).
        -   `LOSE` (if timer runs out or bubbles cross line).

3.  **LEVEL_TRANSITION:**
    -   **Description:** Briefly displays 'LEVEL X COMPLETE! Score: YYYY' or 'GET READY FOR LEVEL X+1!'. Prepares the next level's layout.
    -   **Transition Triggers:**
        -   After a fixed delay (e.g., 2-3 seconds).
        -   If current level was the final level.
    -   **Transition To:**
        -   `PLAYING` (for the next level).
        -   `WIN` (if all levels are completed).

4.  **WIN:**
    -   **Description:** Displays 'YOU WIN!' message and final score. Indicates successful completion of all levels. Offers option to 'Play Again'.
    -   **Transition Trigger:** Player clicks 'Play Again' button.
    -   **Transition To:** `MAIN_MENU` (resets game for a new playthrough).

5.  **LOSE:**
    -   **Description:** Displays 'GAME OVER!' or 'YOU LOSE!' message and final score. Offers option to 'Try Again' or 'Main Menu'.
    -   **Transition Trigger:** Player clicks 'Try Again' or 'Main Menu' button.
    -   **Transition To:** `PLAYING` (restarts current level) or `MAIN_MENU`.

6.  **GAME_OVER:**
    -   **Description:** (Combined with WIN/LOSE for simplicity). The final screen after either winning all levels or losing a level, showing the final score and options to restart or go to the main menu. This state encapsulates the post-game experience.",
  "win_conditions": "**Level Win Condition:**
-   All bubbles currently on the main game board are cleared (popped or detached) *before* the level's timer reaches zero. 

**Overall Game Win Condition:**
-   The player successfully completes (clears all bubbles within the time limit) the final predefined level.",
  "lose_conditions": "**Level Lose Conditions:**
-   The level's timer reaches zero, and there are still bubbles remaining on the game board.
-   Any bubble on the game board crosses a designated 'lose line' (an invisible horizontal line) located just above the shooter's area. If a newly attached bubble or a falling bubble crosses this line, the level is lost.",
  "scoring_system": "The scoring system rewards efficient bubble clearing and speedy level completion.

-   **Popping Bubbles:** Each bubble popped as part of a match (3 or more of the same color) awards **+10 points**.
-   **Detached Bubbles Bonus:** When bubbles are detached from the main cluster and fall off the screen, each detached bubble awards **+20 points** (double the normal pop points).
-   **Time Bonus:** Upon successful completion of a level (all bubbles cleared), the remaining time on the timer is converted into bonus points: **+5 points per second remaining**.

-   **Bonus Points/Multipliers:** No complex multipliers beyond the detached bubble bonus for simplification.
-   **High Score System:** The game will track the top 5 highest scores locally using `localStorage`. After a game ends (win or lose), the player's final score will be compared against the saved high scores, and if it qualifies, the player will be prompted to enter their name.
-   **Score Display:** The current player's score will be continuously displayed in the **top-left corner of the screen** in the format: `SCORE: XXXXX`.
-   **Score Affects Gameplay:** The score directly contributes to the player's final ranking and is the primary metric for success. High scores encourage players to clear boards quickly and efficiently to maximize time bonuses and aim for large detachments. It does not directly alter mechanics like bubble speed or power-ups.",
  "level_system": "The game will feature 3 distinct levels, each progressively increasing in difficulty.

**Level 1: The Basics (Easy Clear)**
-   **Difficulty:** Introductory. Designed to teach core mechanics.
-   **Differences:** 
    -   **Layout:** Relatively sparse bubble layout with clear, easy-to-target clusters of 3-4 bubbles of the same color. Fewer distinct colors (e.g., 3-4 colors).
    -   **Complexity:** Few, if any, bubbles requiring bank shots. Most matches are straightforward direct hits.
    -   **Time Limit:** Generous (e.g., 90 seconds).
    -   **Lose Line:** Slightly lower, providing more breathing room.
-   **Advancement:** Clear all bubbles within 90 seconds. Display: 'LEVEL 1 COMPLETE!' then 'GET READY FOR LEVEL 2!'
-   **Level Indicator:** `LEVEL: 1` displayed in the top-right corner.

**Level 2: Strategic Burst (Intermediate Challenge)**
-   **Difficulty:** Requires more strategic thinking and careful aiming.
-   **Differences:** 
    -   **Layout:** Denser bubble layout, more scattered colors (e.g., 4-5 colors). Some clusters might be partially hidden or require bouncing off walls to reach.
    -   **Complexity:** Introduces sections where popping one color leads to large detachments of another. A higher chance of needing bank shots.
    -   **Time Limit:** Moderate (e.g., 75 seconds).
    -   **Lose Line:** Slightly higher than Level 1, increasing pressure.
-   **Advancement:** Clear all bubbles within 75 seconds. Display: 'LEVEL 2 COMPLETE!' then 'GET READY FOR LEVEL 3!'
-   **Level Indicator:** `LEVEL: 2` displayed in the top-right corner.

**Level 3: Grand Challenge (Expert Puzzler)**
-   **Difficulty:** Significant challenge, requiring mastery of aiming and quick decision-making.
-   **Differences:** 
    -   **Layout:** Very dense bubble layout, maximal distinct colors (e.g., 5-6 colors). Intricate patterns and clusters. Many bubbles are strategically placed to block easy access to others, demanding precise bank shots or careful sequencing of pops.
    -   **Complexity:** Layouts might include 'hanging' sections only supported by a single, hidden bubble, leading to massive potential detachments. More complex 'trap' areas where misfires lead to bubbles crossing the lose line quickly.
    -   **Time Limit:** Tight (e.g., 60 seconds).
    -   **Lose Line:** Highest position, offering minimal error margin.
-   **Advancement:** Clear all bubbles within 60 seconds. Display: 'YOU WIN! Final Score: XXXXX' then transition to `GAME_OVER` screen.
-   **Level Indicator:** `LEVEL: 3` displayed in the top-right corner.",
  "simplifications": "The original description of 'Bubble Cash' implies a complex competitive multiplayer casino game. For an HTML5 implementation using p5.js/Matter.js, several simplifications are necessary:

-   **Multiplayer Competition:** The core 'real-time multiplayer tournaments' and 'rank in the top 3' features are simplified to a **single-player experience**. The player competes against the timer and their own high score, rather than other live players. The 'ranking' concept is abstracted into achieving a high score and completing all levels efficiently.
-   **Casino/Monetization Aspects:** All 'cash' or gambling-related elements, virtual currencies, leaderboards beyond local high scores, and competitive Ladders are completely **removed**.
-   **Pre-arranged Layouts:** While the original mentions 'identical, pre-arranged bubble layout' per match, this is simplified for single-player by having **fixed, static layouts for each defined level** rather than dynamically generated or served layouts per 'match'.
-   **Complex AI/Opponents:** No AI opponents are implemented. The challenge comes from the level design and time constraints.
-   **User Interface Complexity:** The UI will be minimalist, focusing on essential game information (score, timer, level) and game state messages, without intricate menus or social features.
-   **Power-ups/Special Bubbles:** No special power-up bubbles (e.g., bomb bubbles, rainbow bubbles) are included for initial implementation to keep mechanics focused on core matching and physics.
-   **Progression System:** Simplified from a 'tournament progression' to a linear 'level completion' progression. Completing all levels constitutes 'winning' the game.",
  "implementation_notes": "This game will be implemented using p5.js for drawing, game loop management, and user interaction (mouse/keyboard handling). Matter.js will be integrated for robust physics simulations, specifically for bubble collisions (projectile vs. grid, detached bubbles falling) and potentially for the initial placement stability of the bubble grid itself. 

**Key Implementation Details:**
-   **Bubble Grid Management:** A 2D array or similar structure will manage the `Bubble` objects on the board. Each `Bubble` object will store its color, grid coordinates, and a reference to its `Matter.js` body. Custom collision filtering in Matter.js will be crucial to distinguish between static grid bubbles and the dynamic projectile.
-   **Projectile Launch:** A `Matter.Body` will be created for the projectile, and `Matter.Body.setVelocity()` will be used to launch it. Collision detection between the projectile and other static bubbles will trigger the attachment logic.
-   **Attachment and Popping:** When the projectile attaches, its `Matter.Body` will be made static and added to the grid. A recursive or breadth-first search algorithm will identify connected bubbles of the same color for popping. Detached bubbles will have their `Matter.Body` properties updated to enable gravity and collisions with the 'lose line' or bottom of the screen.
-   **Timer:** A simple `millis()` or `frameCount` based countdown timer will manage the `timerRemaining` for each level.
-   **Aiming Guide:** The aiming guide will use `p5.js` vector math to calculate reflections off the canvas edges, simulating a laser guide.
-   **Score and Level Display:** Score will be displayed at `(10, 30)` and Level at `(width - 100, 30)` using `p5.js` `text()` function. The timer will be centered at the top `(width/2, 30)`. All UI text will be rendered in a clear, readable font (e.g., Arial, 24px, white).
-   **Level Progression:** Upon successful completion of a level, a transition screen will display for 2-3 seconds before loading the next level's bubble layout and resetting the timer and score (only for the current level's time bonus, overall score persists).
-   **Game Over/Win Screens:** These states will halt gameplay, display final scores, and offer clear options to restart or return to the main menu.
-   **High Score Storage:** `localStorage` will be used to persist the high score table locally across browser sessions."
}
game_info.description: Welcome to Bubble Cash! Clear all the colored bubbles from the board before time runs out. Aim your bubble shooter to match 3 or more bubbles of the same color to pop them. Make strategic shots to create chain reactions and cause bubbles to detach and fall for bonus points. Complete all 3 increasingly challenging levels to win the game!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left: Rotate shooter left
Arrow Right: Rotate shooter right
Space: Fire bubble
Z: Swap current bubble with next bubble
Enter: Start game (from start screen)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (return to start screen)
